events.text: Patient is a 44-year-old Asian female with a BMI of 21.4. Non-smoker. No significant family history. Prior history of fibrocystic breast changes.

At age 42, patient had routine screening mammogram that detected a 1.2 cm mass in the left breast upper outer quadrant (C50.4). Biopsy confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma (8500/3), grade 2, ER positive (Allred 7/8), PR positive (Allred 6/8), HER2 IHC 2+ with FISH amplified (ratio 2.4), Ki-67 28%.

At age 42, left breast core needle biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 2. ER positive 80%, PR positive 50%, HER2 IHC 2+ with FISH amplified (HER2/CEP17 ratio 2.4, average HER2 copy number 6.8). Ki-67 28%. No lymphovascular invasion.
At age 42, mammogram showed a 1.2 cm oval mass with indistinct margins in the left upper outer quadrant, BIRADS 4B. Ultrasound showed a solid hypoechoic mass measuring 1.3 cm.
At age 42, stage IA (T1cN0M0) left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, HER2-positive, ER/PR-positive.
At age 42, underwent left breast lumpectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy. Final pathology: 1.3 cm IDC, margins negative (closest margin 4 mm), 0 of 3 sentinel lymph nodes positive.
At age 42, received adjuvant paclitaxel weekly for 12 weeks with trastuzumab (TH regimen per APT trial) followed by trastuzumab to complete 1 year.
At age 42, adjuvant whole breast radiation, 40 Gy in 15 fractions (hypofractionated).
At age 43, completed adjuvant trastuzumab. Initiated adjuvant tamoxifen 20 mg daily (premenopausal).
At age 42, during weekly paclitaxel/trastuzumab: grade 1 alopecia, grade 1 fatigue, no significant neuropathy. LVEF stable at 63%.
At age 43, routine surveillance. Patient well on tamoxifen. Hot flashes grade 1. Exam unremarkable. Annual mammography planned.
At age 43, surveillance mammogram of bilateral breasts showed no suspicious findings. Post-lumpectomy changes left breast, stable.
At age 44, patient presents with new persistent dry cough and mild dyspnea on exertion for 3 weeks.
At age 44, CT chest showed multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules (largest 1.8 cm right lower lobe) and a 2.0 cm left adrenal mass. Suspicious for metastatic disease.

At age 44, CT-guided lung biopsy of right lower lobe nodule: metastatic carcinoma consistent with breast primary. ER positive 70%, PR weakly positive 10%, HER2 IHC 3+ (increased from original 2+), Ki-67 40%.
At age 44, recurrent metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer with pulmonary and adrenal metastases. Disease-free interval 2 years.
At age 44, Guardant360 liquid biopsy: ERBB2 amplification, ESR1 D538G (acquired on tamoxifen), PIK3CA E542K, TP53 C176Y. TMB 6 mutations/Mb.
At age 44, initiated first-line for metastatic disease: THP (docetaxel, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) every 21 days, given recurrence more than 12 months after adjuvant trastuzumab.
At age 44, baseline LVEF 60%. After cycle 4 of THP, LVEF 56%. Grade 2 nausea and grade 2 peripheral neuropathy developed.
At age 44, restaging CT after 4 cycles showed partial response with decreasing pulmonary nodules and stable adrenal lesion.
At age 44, after 6 cycles of THP, switched to maintenance HP with fulvestrant 500 mg (given ESR1 mutation rendering tamoxifen and aromatase inhibitors less effective).
At age 44, responding to treatment. Switched endocrine backbone to fulvestrant based on ESR1 mutation. ECOG 0. Will continue HP plus fulvestrant with surveillance every 3 months.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 42, left breast core needle biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 2. ER positive 80%, PR positive 50%, HER2 IHC 2+ with FISH amplified (HER2/CEP17 ratio 2.4, average HER2 copy number 6.8). Ki-67 28%. No lymphovascular invasion.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 42, mammogram showed a 1.2 cm oval mass with indistinct margins in the left upper outer quadrant, BIRADS 4B. Ultrasound showed a solid hypoechoic mass measuring 1.3 cm.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 43, routine surveillance. Patient well on tamoxifen. Hot flashes grade 1. Exam unremarkable. Annual mammography planned.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 43, surveillance mammogram of bilateral breasts showed no suspicious findings. Post-lumpectomy changes left breast, stable.

Please correlate clinically.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

FINDINGS:
At age 44, CT chest showed multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules (largest 1.8 cm right lower lobe) and a 2.0 cm left adrenal mass. Suspicious for metastatic disease.

At age 44, CT-guided lung biopsy of right lower lobe nodule: metastatic carcinoma consistent with breast primary. ER positive 70%, PR weakly positive 10%, HER2 IHC 3+ (increased from original 2+), Ki-67 40%.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 44, Guardant360 liquid biopsy: ERBB2 amplification, ESR1 D538G (acquired on tamoxifen), PIK3CA E542K, TP53 C176Y. TMB 6 mutations/Mb.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 44, restaging CT after 4 cycles showed partial response with decreasing pulmonary nodules and stable adrenal lesion.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 44, responding to treatment. Switched endocrine backbone to fulvestrant based on ESR1 mutation. ECOG 0. Will continue HP plus fulvestrant with surveillance every 3 months.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
